file: The Repository Pattern explained for EVERYONE (with Code Examples) 🚀
brief: Repositories also support the purpose of separating, clearly, in one direction, the dependency between the work domain and the data allocation or mapping. The repository is or repositories are classes or components that encapsulate the logic required to access data sources, all right? They centralize common data access functionality, providing better maintainability and decoupling the infrastructure or technology used to access databases from the domain model layer. I usually call this stuff service, it is a web service, in essence, so that's what I do, and now the really nice thing is that we have no so-called fat controller anymore, the controller has not a lot of logic here, it's just for what the request from the client to the actual repository, and then returns the actual data.
